title: SSRF, DNS Rebinding, Injection, Response Validation, and Least Authority · Go Client Systems · Go Build Room
summary: A platform SDK team integrating a staging control plane must build a first protocol model from narrated fixtures and explain each policy boundary for ssrf, dns rebinding, injection, response validation, and least authority, producing defense-in-depth outbound request policy. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a Go 1.26 net/http defense-in-depth outbound request policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-c…
objectives: Allow-list required schemes, ports, hosts, and destinations and enforce network egress limits for server-side fetching. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Address DNS rebinding and validation-to-connect races by binding policy to resolved and connected destinations. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Reject control characters and unsafe dynamic structure in targets and fields and rely on maintained encoders. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ssrf Destination Policy
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Go staging control-plane client receives https://127.0.0.1:8003/meta, a decimal IP alias, a hostname that rebinds, and a redirect to file://. Enforce scheme, resolution, connected-address, redirect, response, egress, and privacy defenses.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ssrf Destination Policy.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for allow-list required schemes, ports, hosts, and destinations and enforce network egress limits for server-side fetching. demonstrate the result through go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, roundtripper, and httptest evidence. For Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ssrf Destination Policy, keep Ssrf Destination Policy connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

This does not satisfy Ssrf Destination Policy for a Go 1.26 net/http defense-in-depth outbound request policy.
